\chapter*{Experiments for AET-RAG}

Reproducible code for the experimental section of \emph{Adaptive Event-Triggered
Synchronization in Logistics Digital Twins: A RAG-Driven Framework for
Unstructured Data}.

\section*{Layout}

\texttt{
experiments/
  requirements.txt
  config.yaml
  src/
    download\_data.py     \# fetch SVRPBench (HF or GitHub) or fall back to synthetic
    data\_loader.py       \# Instance dataclass + SVRPBench/synthetic loader
    event\_generator.py   \# numerical disruption -$>$ driver-style textual events
    rag\_extractor.py     \# Oracle + optional OpenAI LLM extractor (pydantic-validated)
    aet\_controller.py    \# U/S/R, D\_t, theta\_t, trigger decision
    ortools\_solver.py    \# CVRPTW wrapper around Google OR-Tools
    baselines.py         \# static / continuous / AET-RAG scenarios
    metrics.py           \# per-scenario + aggregate metrics
    run\_experiments.py   \# CLI orchestrator
    plot\_results.py      \# figures referenced in paper/root.tex
  data/                  \# SVRPBench cache (populated by download\_data)
  outputs/
    raw/                 \# per-instance event logs, per-scenario per-event logs
    metrics/             \# summary.csv, aggregate.csv, solver\_call\_reduction.csv
    figures/             \# PDFs mirrored into ../paper/imgs/
}

\section*{Quickstart}

```bash
cd experiments
pip install -r requirements.txt

\chapter*{(optional) download SVRPBench; on failure the pipeline falls back to synthetic}

python -m src.download\_data --config config.yaml

\chapter*{tiny smoke run to validate the pipeline (1 instance, \textasciitilde{}5 events)}

python -m src.run\_experiments --config config.yaml --smoke

\chapter*{full run}

python -m src.run\_experiments --config config.yaml --scenario all

\chapter*{build the figures referenced in paper/root.tex}

python -m src.plot\_results
```

\section*{Reproducibility notes}

\begin{itemize}
\item All randomness flows from \texttt{experiment.seed} in \texttt{config.yaml}.
\item The same event stream is fed to \textbf{all three} scenarios (static, continuous,
AET-RAG) so differences can be attributed to synchronization policy only.
\item \texttt{rag.mode: oracle} runs without any external API: ground-truth severity is
perturbed with controlled Gaussian noise to isolate the controller from
LLM-side variability. Switch to \texttt{llm} (and export \texttt{OPENAI\_API\_KEY}) to obtain
the RAG extraction-quality row of Table III.
\item Hyperparameters (\texttt{theta\_0}, \texttt{theta\_min}, \texttt{gamma}, weights) are exposed in
\texttt{config.yaml}; the sensitivity sweep is performed by editing this file or
passing alternative configs.
\end{itemize}

\section*{Outputs that feed the paper}

$\vert$ Paper artifact (root.tex)          $\vert$ Produced by                                $\vert$
$\vert$ ---------------------------------- $\vert$ ------------------------------------------ $\vert$
$\vert$ Table I \texttt{tab:main\_results}         $\vert$ \texttt{outputs/metrics/aggregate.csv}            $\vert$
$\vert$ Table II \texttt{tab:ablation}            $\vert$ re-runs with edited \texttt{aet.weights}/\texttt{theta\_t}$\vert$
$\vert$ Table III \texttt{tab:rag\_quality}        $\vert$ re-runs with \texttt{rag.mode} in \{oracle, llm\}   $\vert$
$\vert$ Fig. \texttt{fig:solver\_calls}            $\vert$ \texttt{plot\_results.fig\_solver\_calls}            $\vert$
$\vert$ Fig. \texttt{fig:threshold\_trace}         $\vert$ \texttt{plot\_results.fig\_threshold\_trace}         $\vert$
$\vert$ Fig. \texttt{fig:tw\_violations}           $\vert$ \texttt{plot\_results.fig\_tw\_violations}           $\vert$
$\vert$ Fig. \texttt{fig:tradeoff}                $\vert$ \texttt{plot\_results.fig\_tradeoff}                $\vert$
$\vert$ Fig. \texttt{fig:sensitivity}             $\vert$ sweep + custom plotting                    $\vert$
